mnemonics: CRIB Evaluation: Continuous Review Informs Better design (C-R-I-B)
Think F.A.S.T: Feedback And Systematic Testing for continuous improvement
keyPoints: Continuous Internal Evaluation (CIE) is an ongoing assessment process integrated into the algorithm design course, not a single high-stakes exam.
CIE methods include regular quizzes, assignments, code implementations, and project milestones to assess understanding incrementally.
The primary goal is to provide frequent feedback, allowing students and instructors to identify and address weaknesses in understanding algorithmic concepts early.
commonMistakes: Confusing CIE with a single mid-term exam; it is a series of assessments throughout the module.
Focusing only on the final output/code and neglecting to document the design process, choice of algorithm, and complexity analysis.
Underestimating the importance of theoretical proofs (e.g., correctness, optimality) in assignments, focusing solely on practical implementation.
